title: Talk:Yoav Gallant
wikitext: {{Talk header}} {{WikiProject banner shell|class=Start|blp=activepol|listas=Galant, Yoav|1= {{WikiProject Biography|military-work-group=y|military-priority=Low|politician-work-group=y}} {{WikiProject Israel|importance=mid}} {{WikiProject Military history|class=C|b1=n|b2=y|b3=y|b4=y|b5=y|Biography=y|Middle-Eastern=y|Cold-War=y|Post-Cold-War=y}} {{WikiProject Politics}} }} {{Arab-Israeli Arbitration Enforcement}} == Materials == Some relevant articles I haven't had time to go over: * http://www.ynetnews.com/articles/0,7340,L-3943296,00.html * http://www.jpost.com/Features/FrontLines/Article.aspx?id=186124 —[[User:Ynhockey|Ynhockey]] ([[User talk:Ynhockey|Talk]]) 13:07, 27 August 2010 (UTC) == Eyal Erez == "In August 2014 Eyal Erez called the Israeli decisions makers to appoint General Galant to the next IDF Chief of General Staff, after the term of LTG Benny Gantz[3]" This sentence is not supported by the evidence cited.[[User:Johann Nepomuk|Johannes]] ([[User talk:Johann Nepomuk|talk]]) 11:52, 17 March 2015 (UTC). == Lengthened detail section == -not just for Yoav Galant, all Israeli ministers suggesting that the expanded detail section used for ministers or secretaries in all other governments, and for Israeli ministers in the Hebrew language Wikipedia, should also be used for Israeli politicians in the English wiki. People come to wiki for information, and that information that’s presented initially is very relevant, even if it’s a bit long due to the Israeli political system. There doesn’t seem to be another place on the page to present this information clearly [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 22:35, 29 December 2022 (UTC) :As i mentioned on the User talk page, i think we should keep the subterm template. I'm pretty sure it was even exclusively made for Israeli elections. Regardless of whether that's true, israeli politics tend to change very rapidly. [[Tzachi Hanegbi]] served in tons of ministerial portfolios, as did [[Avigdor Liberman]], or [[Aryeh Deri]]. [[Orly Levy]] was in many political parties, and politicians like [[Meir Porush]] served in many different parliamentary spells. The information tends to be relevant, it includes cabinet positions, parliamentary positions and party affiliation. By using the subterm template, we condense the information to be more readable, since even if we could use the normal template for all of it (which we likely can't), we'd still be creating an unreadable clutter. I'd love to hear more opinions though. [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 22:39, 29 December 2022 (UTC) ::Another option is to put another drop-down section by “political career” with all the info, and have condensed info in the initial section [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 22:46, 29 December 2022 (UTC) :::I'm cool with this, i believe its done on [[Shimon Peres]]. Regardless, the infobox should keep the subterm template. [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 22:47, 29 December 2022 (UTC) :::Or a second tab right below the first with ministerial positions expanded [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 23:22, 29 December 2022 (UTC) :::I think at least the current term should be in long form, with a section for previous positions in short form [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 01:49, 30 December 2022 (UTC) Israeli ministers and MKs always have their ministerial offices and Knesset membership listed using the suboffice/subterm parameters. The only exceptions are prime ministers and presidents, who have those offices listed in the main format. Once you start adding in predessors, successors, Prime Minister at the time, etc etc, the infobox gets far too large. That sort of information also usually fails [[MOS:INFOBOXPURPOSE]] (which states that the infobox should be a summary of the article and not introduce new information – predecessors and successors are rarely (if ever) mentioned in such articles. However, there is usually a template footer with all ministers listed in order that readers can get the information from if they want. [[User:Number 57|Number]] [[User talk:Number 57|5]][[Special:Contributions/Number 57|7]] 22:51, 29 December 2022 (UTC) :Then most English language minister/secretary pages should be edited to stop this failure of infoboxpurpose [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 23:52, 29 December 2022 (UTC) ::Most English language politician pages are about people with a handful of party affiliations or positions, they're also none of our buisness. [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 06:41, 30 December 2022 (UTC) :::Right, but apparently no one actually cares that it’s a violation of the rules. That’s the point. [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 15:00, 30 December 2022 (UTC) ::::Its not a violation of the rules though, because firstly, wikipedia doesn't have that many rules, mostly guidelines, and its up to editors working on a certain article or set of articles to reach a consensus. And secondly, because in most cases, politicians in other countries hold fewer positions and fewer party affiliations, which means there's no need to condense information. I frankly don't get your point. [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 15:06, 30 December 2022 (UTC) :::::I don’t think anyone was talking about party affiliation. In your first section you mentioned it would fail the guidelines. Now that that’s been cleared up, I think the best way to do it would be to put a second info box right below the first with ministerial positions. [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 15:10, 30 December 2022 (UTC) ::::::We don't need a second infobox. There are a batch of templates at the bottom of the article which show who held the ministerial offices before and after Galant. [[User:Number 57|Number]] [[User talk:Number 57|5]][[Special:Contributions/Number 57|7]] 16:21, 30 December 2022 (UTC) :::::::I don’t see such a template on any of the pages, and regardless, that’s basic info that should be front and center [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 18:45, 30 December 2022 (UTC) ::::::::They are at the bottom of the article, starting with Current members of the Knesset and ending with Military Secretaries to the Prime Minister. And it's not basic info. Basic info is the person's own positions, their DOB etc. [[User:Number 57|Number]] [[User talk:Number 57|5]][[Special:Contributions/Number 57|7]] 18:53, 30 December 2022 (UTC) :::::::::In addition, British MP’s seem to have the same problem, with many secretariats and such, yet they do have full detail sections in the infobox [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 22:22, 1 January 2023 (UTC) ::::::::::British MPs tend to stick to their party. They also don't hold nearly as many cabinet positions. For comparison, [[Tzachi Hanegbi]], who was until recently one of the most senior MKs, served in 12 different cabinet roles and 3 different parliamentary spells. [[Peter Bottomley]], the most Senior member of the House of Commons, has had two spells and four cabinet positions. [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 22:27, 1 January 2023 (UTC) :::::::::::certainly for current office, they should have full sections with start dates and predecessors etc [[User:Helpfulguy101|Helpfulguy101]] ([[User talk:Helpfulguy101|talk]]) 17:05, 5 March 2023 (UTC) == Gallant or Galant == most other languages spell him Galant. --[[User:Kuhni74|Kuhni74]] ([[User talk:Kuhni74|talk]]) 18:32, 7 October 2023 (UTC) == "We will eliminate everything" == This is a mistranslation and misinformation. The actual quote is {{tq|Gaza will not return to what it was before. There will be no Hamas. We will eliminate it all.}} [https://www.theatlantic.com/international/archive/2024/01/israel-south-africa-genocide-case-fake-quotes/677198/?gift=io6YtOnzuLCwQZEnaHP4G9oAcXYhLiwGtWOE-Sj-9KA&utm_source=copy-link&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=share] --[[User:Hob Gadling|Hob Gadling]] ([[User talk:Hob Gadling|talk]]) 11:30, 23 January 2024 (UTC) == human animals == {{tq|using the phrase "human animals" in reference to Palestinians}} He actually said "We are fighting human animals". Again, someone is confusing Palestinians and Hamas. --[[User:Hob Gadling|Hob Gadling]] ([[User talk:Hob Gadling|talk]]) 10:15, 28 January 2024 (UTC) == Semi-protected edit request on 20 May 2024 == {{Edit semi-protected|Yoav Gallant|answered=yes}} Add the mention of the arrest warrant given against him by ICC [[User:Shantanushinde5|Shantanushinde5]] ([[User talk:Shantanushinde5|talk]]) 12:55, 20 May 2024 (UTC) :[[File:Pictogram voting wait.svg|20px|link=|alt=]] '''Already done''' Already added at the end of the "Minister of Defense" section. [[User:Liu1126|Liu1126]] ([[User talk:Liu1126|talk]]) 13:49, 20 May 2024 (UTC) == Shouldnt this article be extended-confirmed locked? == As is done in all other articles pertaining to Palestine/Israel [[User:Oneequalsequalsone|Oneequalsequalsone]] ([[User talk:Oneequalsequalsone|talk]] · [[Special:Contributions/Oneequalsequalsone|contribs]]) 13:25, 30 June 2024 (UTC) :It probably should, you can ask for that increased protection here - [[Wikipedia:Requests_for_page_protection/Increase]] [[User:Kentucky Rain24|Kentucky Rain24]] ([[User talk:Kentucky Rain24|talk]]) 18:43, 30 June 2024 (UTC) == He has been fired == Should be simple "is" to 'was' change https://www.jpost.com/breaking-news/article-827716 [[User:Jjbomb|Jjbomb]] ([[User talk:Jjbomb|talk]]) 18:14, 5 November 2024 (UTC) == Semi-protected edit request on 5 November 2024 == {{Edit semi-protected|Yoav Gallant|answered=yes}} Yoav Gallant was unexpectedly fired from his post as Minister of Defence by Benjamin Netenyahu on the 5th December 2024. [[Special:Contributions/77.98.5.125|77.98.5.125]] ([[User talk:77.98.5.125|talk]]) 18:25, 5 November 2024 (UTC) :[[File:Red question icon with gradient background.svg|20px|link=|alt=]] '''Not done''': it's not clear what changes you want to be made. Please mention the specific changes in a [[WP:EDITXY|"change X to Y" format]] and provide a [[Wikipedia:Reliable sources|reliable source]] if appropriate. [[User:M.Bitton|M.Bitton]] ([[User talk:M.Bitton|talk]]) 18:57, 5 November 2024 (UTC) == [[WP:BLPCRIME]] == Categories like "war criminal" and "fugitive" don't seem to be BLPCRIME-compliant '''[[User:AndreJustAndre|Andre]]'''[[User_talk:AndreJustAndre|🚐]] 20:51, 13 December 2024 (UTC) :While my initial thought was that BLPCRIME might not apply in relation to international criminal law, it's a very valid question that may effect other articles as well. :As such, I've asked at [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view/Noticeboard|WP:NPOVN]] about [[Wikipedia:Neutral point of view/Noticeboard#WP:BLPCRIME & international criminal law|WP:BLPCRIME & international criminal law]] [[Wikipedia:Biographies_of_living_persons/Noticeboard#WP:BLPCRIME_&_international_criminal_law|WP:BLPCRIME & international criminal law]]. - [[User:Butterscotch Beluga|Butterscotch Beluga]] ([[User talk:Butterscotch Beluga|talk]]) 23:03, 13 December 2024 (UTC) ::Thank you for doing that. Based on the points by Notwally, I think these cats should clearly be removed. We can leave the discussion open for a little bit but this is a BLP violation. [00:40, 14 December 2024 (UTC)] ::I also think describing the arrest warrant in the lead is too much weight and prominence for this item '''[[User:AndreJustAndre|Andre]]'''[[User_talk:AndreJustAndre|🚐]] 02:17, 14 December 2024 (UTC) :::I'm fine with the removal of categories, but I think the arrest warrant should stay in the lead. It's rather notable to be issued an arrest warrant by the ICC & it's mentioned in the lead of almost every other person who's been issued one, including but not limited to [[Vladimir Putin]], [[Joseph Kony]], [[Maria Lvova-Belova]], [[Noureddine Adam]], [[Abdallah Banda]], & [[Mohammed Deif]]. [[User:Butterscotch Beluga|Butterscotch Beluga]] ([[User talk:Butterscotch Beluga|talk]]) 03:00, 14 December 2024 (UTC) == Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 18 December 2024 == {{edit extended-protected|Yoav Gallant|answered=yes}} Change "Yoav Gallant made a public speech in early October 2024 saying, ‘there will be no more electricity, no more food, no more fuel … We are fighting against human animals and will behave accordingly’." to Yoav Gallant made a public speech in early October 2023 saying, ‘there will be no more electricity, no more food, no more fuel … We are fighting against human animals and will behave accordingly’. [[User:DentalHygienist|DentalHygienist]] ([[User talk:DentalHygienist|talk]]) 19:43, 18 December 2024 (UTC) :I removed it entirely as the sentence had duplicated information in the same section but in the wrong place chronologically. Thank you! [[User:ObserveOwl|ObserveOwl 🎄]] ([[User talk:ObserveOwl#top|talk]]) 21:54, 18 December 2024 (UTC) == Starvation as a weapon == The last paragraph detailing his time as defense minister really has a lot of extraneous information: On 21 November 2024, the [[International Criminal Court]] issued [[International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Israeli figures|arrest warrants]] for Gallant, Benjamin Netanyahu, and Hamas military commander [[Mohammed Deif]] for alleged war crimes committed during the [[Israel–Hamas war]]. One of the key allegations in the [[International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Israeli leaders|International Criminal Court arrest warrants]] for Gallant and Netanyahu was the "use of starvation as a weapon of war". The use of starvation as weapon of war is banned by the [[United Nations]]. Since then it has been used in both Gaza and Sudan. In mid 2023 German government officials also accused Russia of using hunger as a weapon. A few weeks before that the arrest warrants were issued, there were also reports of looting occurring in areas controlled by the IDF. I propose changing it to something along the lines of: On 21 November 2024, the International Criminal Court [[International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Israeli leaders|issued arrest warrants for Gallant, Benjamin Netanyahu]], and Hamas military commander [[Mohammed Deif]] for alleged war crimes committed during the [[Israel–Hamas war]]. In the arrest warrants, Gallant, along with Netanyahu, was accused of the using [[Starvation (crime)|"starvation as a weapon of war"]]. Shortly before the arrest warrants were issued, the IDF had recently been accused of allowing the systematic looting of aid shipments by armed gangs in areas of Gaza under its control. [[User:Dmh17|Dmh17]] ([[User talk:Dmh17|talk]]) 05:09, 19 December 2024 (UTC) :{{already done}} (not by me though) :I will remove the sentence "In mid 2023 German government officials also accused Russia of using hunger as a weapon." It is sourced by an opinion piece urging the German government to speak against Israel using hunger as a weapon, just as they did to Russia. This has too little relevance for an article about Gallant. Friendly, [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 13:25, 30 May 2025 (UTC) == Gallant is not an MK anymore == Yoav Gallant has resigned from the Knesset on January 1st, 2025. https://www.reuters.com/world/middle-east/israels-former-defence-chief-gallant-quits-politics-2025-01-01 https://www.timesofisrael.com/liveblog_entry/report-gallant-resigned-in-order-to-prevent-likud-from-ousting-him-from-party/ This means extensive rewriting of the article is required. [[User:Halfling Daniel|Halfling Daniel]] ([[User talk:Halfling Daniel|talk]]) 21:39, 1 January 2025 (UTC) :I don't think it is certain yet. The Times of Israel article includes several other MKs urging him to delay leaving the Knesset, while other sources say that yesterday's statement was than he 'planned' to resign, not that he had. [[User:Number 57|Number]] [[User talk:Number 57|5]][[Special:Contributions/Number 57|7]] 16:13, 2 January 2025 (UTC) == Incorrect date == The first paragraph of the article incorrectly states "Gallant subsequently resigned from the Knesset on 5 January 2024." when it should say 2025 [[User:Person568|Person568]] ([[User talk:Person568|talk]]) 15:49, 17 January 2025 (UTC) :Oops. Overlooked that yesterday, its now fixed. Thank you! [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 16:07, 17 January 2025 (UTC) == Edit request 28 March 2025 == {{Edit extended-protected|answered=yes}} '''Request removal or better sourcing for claim that Yoav Gallant ordered the Hannibal Directive to be implemented during the October 7th attacks.''' I would request that the following passage is either removed or better sourced. The issues are as follows: 1. The first source given is from NDTV, but the link is broken. 2. The second is the Electronic Intifada, listed as an unreliable source here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources/Perennial_sources/3 3. The third is Middle East Monitor. The article isn't super clear on saying that he issued it (refers to "they") and is also listed here as being a source without consensus on its reliability: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources/Perennial_sources/5 4. The fourth source is Anadolu Agency. The article doesn't say that Gallant ordered it, but in any case is listed as unreliable here for controversial and international topics: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources/Perennial_sources/1#Anadolu_Agency_(controversial_topics) I did try to find a better source for this but could not. Failing a better source being provided, I would request that this be removed. '''Diff:''' {{TextDiff|1=In February 2025, Gallant admitted that he issued the Hannibal Directive during the 7 October attacks. In an interview with Channel 12, he confirmed of giving the order in certain areas. He also insisted on launching a military offensive in Gaza despite warnings that such an attack could endanger the Israeli hostages held there. He stated, “I think that, tactically, in some places, it was given, and in other places, it was not given, and that is a problem."[81][82][83][84]|2=}} [[User:Gomedog|Gomedog]] ([[User talk:Gomedog|talk]]) 21:50, 28 March 2025 (UTC) :[https://www.spectator.co.uk/article/the-audacity-of-trumps-gaza-plan/][https://www.palestinechronicle.com/anything-that-moved-israel-admits-air-force-applied-hannibal-directive-on-oct-7/][https://www.timesofisrael.com/gallant-says-government-didnt-do-everything-it-could-to-bring-back-the-hostages/]? [[User:Talib1101|1101]] ([[User talk:Talib1101|talk]]) 06:17, 29 March 2025 (UTC) ::The only one of these sources that mentions the hannibal doctrine is the Palestine Chronicle, which seems to be dubiously citeable. I think it best if we were to find a more reliable source. Maybe one of these two: https://www.middleeastmonitor.com/20250208-former-israel-defence-minister-admits-issuing-orders-to-attack-gaza-kill-israeli-captives/, https://www.haaretz.com/israel-news/2024-07-07/ty-article-magazine/.premium/idf-ordered-hannibal-directive-on-october-7-to-prevent-hamas-taking-soldiers-captive/00000190-89a2-d776-a3b1-fdbe45520000. The phrasing in both articles is a bit different though, maybe replacing "admitted" with "stated", "insisted" with "decided" and "confirmed of giving" with "confirmed that he had given" [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 10:18, 29 March 2025 (UTC) :::The Middle East Monitor one is the same as that already in the article and does seem to be the strongest source for this, but I wouldn’t use it without consensus from more reliable sources. I would say it should at least be attributed. :::The Haaretz one, unless I missed something, doesn’t seem to say he ordered it but rather it was ordered at different levels and areas of the IDF. From other sources I have seen him say the directive was employed but not that he ordered it. :::Either way, I also think that “admitted” isn’t a neutral POV unless he, for example, denied it was employed in the past. I didn’t find that in a quick look. [[User:Gomedog|Gomedog]] ([[User talk:Gomedog|talk]]) 13:26, 29 March 2025 (UTC) ::::I agree on "admitted" as mentioned earlier and missed the Middle East Monitor line. I think you could attribute the report to "outlets like the middle east monitor" and neutralize the phrasing a little, but idrk if im in favor of complete removal. [[User:Totalstgamer|Totalstgamer]] ([[User talk:Totalstgamer|talk]]) 15:23, 29 March 2025 (UTC) :{{not done}}: an extended confirmed editor objected to the removal. [[User:Perception312|Perception312]] ([[User talk:Perception312|talk]]) 13:34, 8 June 2025 (UTC) == incomplete list of persons targeted by ICC arrest warrant == The current wording is: "On 21 November 2024, the [[International Criminal Court]] [[International Criminal Court arrest warrants for Israeli figures|issued an arrest warrant]] for Gallant along with Netanyahu and [[Hamas]] leader [[Mohammed Deif]] for alleged war crimes and crimes against humanity committed during the [[Gaza war]]." This leaves out that warrants were also issued against Hamas leaders Ismail Haniyeh and Yahya Sinwar. source here: https://www.icc-cpi.int/news/statement-icc-prosecutor-karim-aa-khan-kc-applications-arrest-warrants-situation-state Could an extended confirmed user please add this in? [[User:Kieranvolbrecht|Kieranvolbrecht]] ([[User talk:Kieranvolbrecht|talk]]) 12:49, 1 May 2025 (UTC) :{{done}} Hi [[User:Kieranvolbrecht|Kieranvolbrecht]], I have added the two names to the body of the article, and your source. In the lead, I summarised to "three leaders". Friendly, [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 13:14, 30 May 2025 (UTC) == NOTICE regarding "Fugitive under international law" in lead sentence == If you are seeking to contest/remove "fugitive under international law" from the lead sentence, defer to the results of this discussion: [[Talk:Benjamin Netanyahu#First sentence]] [[User:Alexandraaaacs1989|Alexandraaaacs1989]] ([[User talk:Alexandraaaacs1989|talk]]) 08:14, 1 October 2025 (UTC)

sections.line: Shouldnt this article be extended-confirmed locked?
Gallant is not an MK anymore
incomplete list of persons targeted by ICC arrest warrant
NOTICE regarding "Fugitive under international law" in lead sentence
